item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject the statement from their first to final turns. Agent A frames opposition through risks to minorities, weaker people, dignity, and abuse of power in Indonesia; Agent B frames opposition through American rights, due process, free speech, and limits on state or majority power. Both acknowledge that public interest can matter, but only as a limited concern bounded by individual rights, so there is no visible flip or directional drift.
